description: Letter addressed to Yūsuf al-Surrī. The name Saʿīd Maḍmūn the Blind appears at the bottom of verso; unclear if this is the name of the writer. The letter is written in Judaeo-Arabic. The hand looks Yemeni. Dating: Probably Ottoman-era, based on the mention of the currency qirsh. The purpose of the letter is to inform the addressee that the money he kindly sent (or promised to send) never arrived. The writer mentions his paternal aunts Shamʿa and Turkiyya. NB: This IIIF image is currently displaying incorrectly in Figgy (DPUL), for the correct image consult FGP or Ktiv.
description_he: מכתב מופנה ליוסף אל-סורי. שם סעיד מצ'מון העיוור מופיע בתחתית הגב. ביהודית-ערבית. כתב יד ימני. תאריך: ככל הנראה עות'מאני (מוזכר קירש). הכסף שנשלח לא הגיע. מוזכרות דודות שמעא וטורקיה.
